1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ وَٱلصَّـٰٓفَّـٰتِ صَفًّا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I CALL TO witness those who stand arrayed in rows,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: By oath of those who establish proper ranks.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: By the rangers ranging

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: By the angels ranged in ranks.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: By those (angels) ranged in ranks (or rows).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: By the aligners aligning.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: By those who range themselves in rows;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: By those ranged in ranks.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: By those who set the ranks in battle order

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: By the [angels] ranged in ranks,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: By the aligners (angels) aligning.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: By those [angels] lined up in rows

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: I swear by (the angels) who stand in ranks,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: I swear by those who draw themselves out in ranks

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: By those [angels] who range themselves in close ranks

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: By those who range themselves in ranks,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalssaffati saffan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And those who restrain by reprimanding,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And by oath of those who herd with a stern warning.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and the scarers scaring

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: By the angels driving away.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: By those (angels) who drive the clouds in a good way.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the drivers driving.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: by those who reprove severely,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: By those who drive the clouds in a good way.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those who drive away (the wicked) with reproof

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: by the ones who drive [the clouds] vigorously,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and the drivers driving,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who drive [the clouds]

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: by those who drive away the devil (to protect Our revelation),

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then those who drive away with reproof,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and those who drive away [the wicked] with reproof

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And so are strong in repelling (evil),

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faalzzajirati zajran

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And those who recite the Reminder,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And by oath of the groups that read the Qur’an.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and the reciters of a Remembrance,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: By the angels reciting the praise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: By those (angels) who bring the Book and the Quran from Allah to mankind [Tafsir Ibn Kathir].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the reciters of the Reminder.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and those who recite the Exhortation;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: By those who bring the Dhikr.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those who read (the Word) for a reminder,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: by the ones who recite the reminder:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and those who recite the Remembrance

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who recite the message,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and those who recite Our revelations,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then those who recite, being mindful,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and by the reciters of the Reminder:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And thus proclaim the Message (of Allah)!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faalttaliyati thikran

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Verily your God is One,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed your God is surely only One.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: surely your God is One,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily your God is One.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily your Ilah (God) is indeed One (i.e. Allah);

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your God is indeed One.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: surely your God is One,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, your God is indeed One;

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! thy Lord is surely One;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: indeed your God is certainly One,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: surely, your God is One,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, your God is One,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: that your Lord is the only Lord.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely your Allah is One:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: your God is One,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily, verily, your Allah is one!-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna ilahakum lawahidun

5.arabic: رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَرَبُّ ٱلْمَشَـٰرِقِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, and the Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them – and the Lord of the sun’s rising points.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and of what between them is Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in- between the twain, and Lord of the easts.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and of the earth, and all that is between them, and Lord of every point of the sun's risings. (None has the right to be worshipped but Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them; and Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and of whatever lies between the two, the Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and of the earth, and all that is between them, and Lord of every point of the sun's risings.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and of the earth and all that is between them, and Lord of the sun's risings.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and the Lord of the easts.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and of all that is between them; the Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that between them and Lord of the sunrises.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, the Lord of the Eastern regions.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, and Lord of the easts.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything between them; Lord of the Easts.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Lord of the heavens and of the earth and all between them, and Lord of every point at the rising of the sun!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma warabbu almashariqi

6.arabic: إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِزِينَةٍ ٱلْكَوَاكِبِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He decked the nearest heavens with ornaments of stars,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We have indeed adorned the lowest heaven with stars as ornaments.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We have adorned the lower heaven with the adornment of the stars

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily We! We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment: the stars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! We have adorned the near heaven with the stars (for beauty).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We have adorned the lower heaven with the beauty of the planets.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We have adorned the lower heaven with the adornment of the stars

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, We have adorned the near heaven with the stars.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! We have adorned the lowest heaven with an ornament, the planets;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed We have adorned the lowest heaven with the finery of the stars,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have adorned the lower heaven with the adornment of the planets,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment of stars

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have decked the lower heavens with stars

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment, the stars,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have adorned the lowest heaven with the beauty of the planets;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We have indeed decked the lower heaven with beauty (in) the stars,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna zayyanna alssamaa alddunya bizeenatin alkawakibi

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Protecting them against every wayward devil,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to protect it from every rebellious devil.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and to preserve against every rebel Satan;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And have placed therein a guard against any Satan froward.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to guard against every rebellious devil.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And guarded it against every defiant devil.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and have protected it from every rebellious satan.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to guard against every rebellious Shaytan.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: With security from every froward devil.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and to guard from any froward devil.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: a protection against every rebel satan;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And as protection against every rebellious devil

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: to protect them from the rebellious satan.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (there is) a safeguard against every rebellious Shaitan.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and guarded it against all rebellious devils:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (For beauty) and for guard against all obstinate rebellious evil spirits,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wahifthan min kulli shaytanin maridin

8.arabic: لَّا يَسَّمَّعُونَ إِلَى ٱلْمَلَإِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ وَيُقْذَفُونَ مِن كُلِّ جَانِبٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So that they are not able to listen to the angels of higher (echelons), and are pelted from all sides

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They cannot listen to the speech of those on higher elevations and they are targeted from every side.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: they listen not to the High Council, for they are pelted from every side,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They cannot listen to the exalted assembly, and they are darted at from every side.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They cannot listen to the higher group (angels) for they are pelted from every side.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They cannot eavesdrop on the Supernal Elite, for they get bombarded from every side.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: These satans cannot listen to what transpires in the High Council for they are pelted away from every side

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They cannot listen to the higher group for they are pelted from every side.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They cannot listen to the Highest Chiefs for they are pelted from every side,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They do not eavesdrop on the Supernal Elite—they are shot at from every side,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: so they cannot listen to the High Assembly, for they are pelted from every side.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [So] they may not listen to the exalted assembly [of angels] and are pelted from every side,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The devils cannot hear those high above. They would be struck from all sides

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They cannot listen to the exalted assembly and they are thrown at from every side,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: they cannot overhear the Higher Assembly for they are pelted from every side,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (So) they should not strain their ears in the direction of the Exalted Assembly but be cast away from every side,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La yassammaAAoona ila almalai alaAAla wayuqthafoona min kulli janibin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And kept far away. There is perpetual punishment for them,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: To make them flee, and for them is a never-ending punishment.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: rejected, and theirs is an everlasting chastisement,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: With a driving fusillade, and theirs shall be a torment perpetual;

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Outcast, and theirs is a constant (or painful) torment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Repelled—they will have a lingering torment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and are repelled. Theirs is an unceasing chastisement.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Outcast, and theirs is a constant torment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Outcast, and theirs is a perpetual torment;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: to drive them away, and there is a perpetual punishment for them—

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They are rejected and theirs is an everlasting punishment;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Repelled; and for them is a constant punishment,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and driven away to suffer the necessary torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Being driven off, and for them is a perpetual chastisement,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: driven away, and will suffer eternal punishment.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Repulsed, for they are under a perpetual penalty,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Duhooran walahum AAathabun wasibun

10.arabic: إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ ٱلْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٌ ثَاقِبٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Except those who eavesdrop and are pursued by a shooting flame.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Except one who sometimes steals a part, so a blazing flame goes after him.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: except such as snatches a fragment, and he is pursued by a piercing flame.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Except him who snatcheth away a word by stealth, and him then pursueth a glowing flame.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Except such as snatch away something by stealing and they are pursued by a flaming fire of piercing brightness.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except for him who snatches a fragment—he gets pursued by a piercing projectile.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if any is able to snatch a fragment, he is pursued by a piercing flame.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Except such as snatch away something by stealing, and they are pursued by a flaming fire of piercing brightness.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Save him who snatcheth a fragment, and there pursueth him a piercing flame.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: except any who snatches a snatch, whereat a piercing flame pursues him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: except such as snatches a fragment, and he is pursued by a piercing flame.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Except one who snatches [some words] by theft, but they are pursued by a burning flame, piercing [in brightness].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Some of them who stealthily steal words from the heavens are pursued by a glistening flame.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Except him who snatches off but once, then there follows him a brightly shining flame.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But if anyone does succeed in snatching a glimpse [of such knowledge], he shall be pursued by a piercing flame.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Except such as snatch away something by stealth, and they are pursued by a flaming fire, of piercing brightness.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa man khatifa alkhatfata faatbaAAahu shihabun thaqibun

11.arabic: فَٱسْتَفْتِهِمْ أَهُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَم مَّنْ خَلَقْنَآ ۚ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰهُم مِّن طِينٍ لَّازِبٍۭ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So ask them if they are more difficult to create, or the rest We have created? Indeed (man) We created from fermented clay.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore ask them (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Are they a stronger creation, or are other things of our creation, (the angels, the heavens etc.)?” We have indeed created them from sticky clay.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So ask them for a pronouncement -- Are they stronger in constitution, or those We created? We created them of clinging clay.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Ask them thou: are they stronger in structure or those others whom We have created? Verily We! We have created them of a sticky clay.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then ask them (i.e. these polytheists, O Muhammad SAW): "Are they stronger as creation, or those (others like the heavens and the earth and the mountains, etc.) whom We have created?" Verily, We created them of a sticky clay.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Inquire of them, “Are they more difficult to create, or the others We created?” We created them from sticky clay.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So ask them (that is, human beings): “Were they harder to create than the objects We created?” We created them from sticky clay.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then ask them: "Are they harder to create, or those whom We have created" Verily, We created them of a sticky clay.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then ask them (O Muhammad): Are they stronger as a creation, or those (others) whom we have created? Lo! We created them of plastic clay.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Ask them, is their creation more prodigious or [that of other creatures] that We have created? Indeed, We created them from a viscous clay.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So ask them, are they stronger in constitution, or those whom We have created. We have created them of sticky clay.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Are they a stronger [or more difficult] creation or those [others] We have created?" Indeed, We created men from sticky clay.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Have they (people) been created stronger than what We have created?" We have created them from moist clay.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then ask them whether they are stronger in creation or those (others) whom We have created. Surely We created them of firm clay.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So, ask those who deny the truth if it was harder to create them than all the other things We have created? We created them from sticky clay.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Just ask their opinion: are they the more difficult to create, or the (other) beings We have created? Them have We created out of a sticky clay!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faistaftihim ahum ashaddu khalqan am man khalaqna inna khalaqnahum min teenin lazibin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet while you are filled with wonder, they just scoff;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Rather you are surprised, whereas they keep mocking.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Nay, thou marvellest; and they scoff

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Aye! thou marvellest, and they scoff.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Nay, you (O Muhammad SAW) wondered (at their insolence) while they mock (at you and at the Quran).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But you wonder, and they ridicule.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You marvel (at the wondrous creations of Allah) and they scoff at it,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Nay, you wondered while they mock.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nay, but thou dost marvel when they mock

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed you wonder, while they engage in ridicule,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: No, you marvel, while they scoff.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But you wonder, while they mock,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), you will be surprised that they still mock (God's revelations).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nay! you wonder while they mock,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: No wonder you are surprised as they laugh with scorn.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Truly dost thou marvel, while they ridicule,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Bal AAajibta wayaskharoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And when they are warned they pay no heed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they do not understand, when explained to.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and, when reminded, do not remember

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when they are admonished, they receive not admcnition.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when they are reminded, they pay no attention.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when reminded, they pay no attention.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and when they are admonished, they pay no heed;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when they are reminded, they pay no attention.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And heed not when they are reminded,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and [even] when admonished do not take admonition,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When they are reminded, they do not remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when they are reminded, they remember not.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They pay no attention when they are reminded

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when they are reminded, they mind not,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When they are reminded, they do not pay heed,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And, when they are admonished, pay no heed,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha thukkiroo la yathkuroona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When they see a sign, they laugh at it,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And whenever they see a sign, they mock at it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and, when they see a sign, would scoff;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when they behold a sign, they turn to scoffing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when they see an Ayah (a sign, a proof, or an evidence) from Allah, they mock at it.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when they see a sign, they ridicule.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and if they see any Sign, they laugh it away

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when they see an Ayah (a sign) from Allah, they mock at it.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And seek to scoff when they behold a portent.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and when they see a sign they make it an object of ridicule,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When they are shown a sign, they scoff at it

translations.author: Saheeh International

translations.text: They will say: "It was you who used to come to us from the right side [i.e. from the right side of one of us and beautify for us every evil, order us for polytheism, and stop us from the truth i.e. Islamic Monotheism and from every good deed]."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will say, “You used to come at us from the right.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (The followers will say to their leaders): “You used to come to us from the right hand.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will say: "It was you who used to come to us from the right side."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They say: Lo! ye used to come unto us, imposing, (swearing that ye spoke the truth).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will say, ‘Indeed you used to accost us peremptorily.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: saying: 'You used to come to us from the right hand'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will say, "Indeed, you used to come at us from the right."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: "It was you who tried to mislead us from righteousness".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall say: Surely you used to come to us from the right side.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will say, "You used to come at us from the right."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will say: "It was ye who used to come to us from the right hand (of power and authority)!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo innakum kuntum tatoonana AAani alyameeni

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will say: "No, you were the ones who would not believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will answer, “You yourselves did not have faith!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those say, 'No; on the contrary, you were not believers;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will say: nay! ye yourselves were not believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will reply: "Nay, you yourselves were not believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will say, “You yourselves were not believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will say: “Nay, you yourselves were not the ones who would believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will reply: "Nay, you yourselves were not believers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They answer: Nay, but ye (yourselves) were not believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will answer, ‘No, you [yourselves] had no faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But they reply: 'Rather, you were not believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The oppressors will say, "Rather, you [yourselves] were not believers,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Others will respond, "It was you who did not want to have any faith.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall say: Nay, you (yourselves) were not believers;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will say, "No! It was you who would not believe --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will reply: "Nay, ye yourselves had no Faith!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo bal lam takoonoo mumineena

30.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا عَلَيْكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍۭ ۖ بَلْ كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا طَـٰغِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We had no power over you. In fact, you were a people rebellious.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And we did not have any control over you; but in fact you yourselves were rebellious.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: we had no authority over you; no, you were an insolent people.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And we had over you no authority, but ye were a people exorbitant.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And we had no authority over you. Nay! But you were transgressing people (disobedient, polytheists, and disbelievers).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We had no authority over you. You yourselves were rebellious people.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We had no power over you. You were a rebellious people,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And we had no authority over you. Nay! But you were a transgressing people."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We had no power over you, but ye were wayward folk.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We had no authority over you. No, you [yourselves] were a rebellious lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We had no authority over you, but you were an insolent nation.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And we had over you no authority, but you were a transgressing people.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We had no authority over you, in fact, you were a rebellious people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And we had no authority over you, but you were an inordinate people;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: we had no power over you; but you yourselves were a rebellious people.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Nor had we any authority over you. Nay, it was ye who were a people in obstinate rebellion!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kana lana AAalaykum min sultanin bal kuntum qawman tagheena

31.arabic: فَحَقَّ عَلَيْنَا قَوْلُ رَبِّنَآ ۖ إِنَّا لَذَآئِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The sentence of our Lord has come true for us. We have certainly to taste (the punishment),

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “So the Word of our Lord has proved true upon us; we will surely have to taste (the punishment).”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So our Lord's Word is realised against us; we are tasting it.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So on us hath been justified the sentence of our Lord: verily we are to taste.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "So now the Word of our Lord has been justified against us, that we shall certainly (have to) taste (the torment).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The Word of our Lord has been realized against us. We are tasting it.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and so we became deserving of the Word of our Lord that we shall be made to suffer chastisement.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "So now the Word of our Lord has been justified against us, that we shall certainly taste (the torment)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Now the Word of our Lord hath been fulfilled concerning us. Lo! we are about to taste (the doom).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So our Lord’s word became due against us that we shall indeed taste [the punishment].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The statement of Our Lord is realized against us, and we are tasting it,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So the word of our Lord has come into effect upon us; indeed, we will taste [punishment].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Thus, the words of Our Lord about us have come true and now we are suffering the torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So the sentence of our Lord has come to pass against us: (now) we shall surely taste;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But now our Lord's word has come true against us: truly, we are bound to taste [the punishment].

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "So now has been proved true, against us, the word of our Lord that we shall indeed (have to) taste (the punishment of our sins).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fahaqqa AAalayna qawlu rabbina inna lathaiqoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For we had led you astray. In fact we ourselves were astray."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “We therefore led you astray, for we ourselves were astray!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Therefore we perverted you, and we ourselves were perverts.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: We seduced you astray; verily we were ourselves the seduced ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "So we led you astray because we were ourselves astray."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We seduced you. We were seducers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So we led you astray; we ourselves were strayed.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "So we led you astray because we were ourselves astray."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Thus we misled you. Lo! we were (ourselves) astray.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So we perverted you, for we were perverse [ourselves].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: we perverted you, indeed, we were perverts'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And we led you to deviation; indeed, we were deviators."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We mislead you and we ourselves had also gone astray."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So we led you astray, for we ourselves were erring.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We led you astray as we were ourselves astray."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "We led you astray: for truly we were ourselves astray."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faaghwaynakum inna kunna ghaweena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, they will become partners in punishment.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So this day they all are partners in the punishment.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So all of them on that day are sharers in the chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So verily on that Day they all in the torment will be sharers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then verily, that Day, they will (all) share in the torment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: On that Day, they will share in the punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: On that Day, they will all share the chastisement.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then verily, that Day, they will (all) share in the torment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then lo! this day they (both) are sharers in the doom.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So, that day they will share the punishment.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Therefore, on that Day they will all share Our punishment.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So indeed they, that Day, will be sharing in the punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On that day they will all share the torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So they shall on that day be sharers in the chastisement one with another.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: On that Day they will all share the punishment:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Truly, that Day, they will (all) share in the Penalty.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fainnahum yawmaithin fee alAAathabi mushtarikoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That is how We deal with sinners.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is how We deal with the guilty.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Even so We do with the sinners;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily We! in this wise We deal with the culprits.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Certainly, that is how We deal with Al-Mujrimun (polytheists, sinners, criminals, the disobedient to Allah, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Thus We deal with the sinners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus do We treat the culprits.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Certainly, that is how We deal with the criminals.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! thus deal We with the guilty.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed, that is how We deal with the guilty.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As such We shall deal with the evildoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, that is how We deal with the criminals.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is how We deal with the criminals.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely thus do We deal with the guilty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: that is how We deal with evil-doers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily that is how We shall deal with Sinners.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna kathalika nafAAalu bialmujrimeena

35.arabic: إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوٓا۟ إِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They were those who, when it was said to them, "There is no god but God," behaved with insolence,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed, when it was said to them, “There is no God except Allah”, they were haughty.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: for when it was said to them, 'There is no god but God,' they were ever waxing proud,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily when it was said Unto them: there is no god but Allah, they ever grew stiff-necked.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Truly, when it was said to them: La ilaha ill-Allah "(none has the right to be worshipped but Allah)," they puffed themselves up with pride (i.e. denied it).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When it was said to them, “There is no god except God,” they grew arrogant.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Whenever it was said to them: “There is no true deity apart from Allah,” they waxed proud

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Truly, when it was said to them: "La ilaha illallah," they puffed themselves up with pride.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: For when it was said unto them, There is no Allah save Allah, they were scornful

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed, it was they who, when they were told, ‘There is no god except Allah,’ used to be disdainful,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For when it was said to them: 'There is no god except Allah' they were always proud

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed they, when it was said to them, "There is no deity but Allah," were arrogant

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They were the ones, who on being told, "God is only One," become puffed-up with pride

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely they used to behave proudly when it was said to them: There is no god but Allah;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When they were told, "There is no deity but God," they turned away with disdain,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For they, when they were told that there is no god except Allah, would puff themselves up with Pride,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innahum kanoo itha qeela lahum la ilaha illa Allahu yastakbiroona

36.arabic: وَيَقُولُونَ أَئِنَّا لَتَارِكُوٓا۟ ءَالِهَتِنَا لِشَاعِرٍ مَّجْنُونٍۭ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And said: "Should we abandon our gods for the sake of an insane poet?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they used to say, “Shall we forsake our Gods upon the sayings of a mad poet?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: saying, 'What, shall we forsake our gods for a poet possessed?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they said: are we going to abandon our gods on account of a Poet distracted?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (they) said: "Are we going to abandon our aliha (gods) for the sake of a mad poet?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And said, “Are we to abandon our gods for a mad poet?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and said: “Shall we forsake our deities for the sake of a distracted poet?”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (they) said: "Are we going to abandon our gods for the sake of a mad poet"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And said: Shall we forsake our gods for a mad poet?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and [they would] say, ‘Shall we abandon our gods for a crazy poet?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and said: 'Are we to renounce our gods for the sake of a mad poet'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And were saying, "Are we to leave our gods for a mad poet?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and said, "Should we give up our idols for the sake of an insane poet".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to say: What! shall we indeed give up our gods for the sake of a mad poet?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and replied, "Shall we then give up our deities at the bidding of a mad poet?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And say: "What! shall we give up our gods for the sake of a Poet possessed?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayaqooloona ainna latarikoo alihatina lishaAAirin majnoonin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Not in the least. He has brought the truth and confirmed the other messengers.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In fact he has brought the Truth, and testified for the Noble Messengers!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'No indeed; but he brought the truth, and confirmed the Envoys.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Aye! he hath come with the truth, and he confesseth to the sent ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Nay! he (Muhammad SAW) has come with the truth (i.e. Allah's Religion - Islamic Monotheism and this Quran) and he confirms the Messengers (before him who brought Allah's religion - Islamic Monotheism).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In fact, he came with the truth, and he confirmed the messengers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (They say so although) he brought the Truth and confirmed the veracity of the Messengers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Nay! he has come with the truth and he confirms the Messengers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nay, but he brought the Truth, and he confirmed those sent (before him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed, he has brought [them] the truth, and confirmed the [earlier] apostles.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: No, indeed, he has brought the truth, and confirmed the Messengers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Rather, the Prophet has come with the truth and confirmed the [previous] messengers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: In fact, he had brought them the truth and had acknowledged the Messengers (who were sent before him).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nay: he has come with the truth and verified the apostles.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Surely, he has brought the truth, confirming those who were sent before;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nay! he has come with the (very) Truth, and he confirms (the Message of) the messengers (before him).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Bal jaa bialhaqqi wasaddaqa almursaleena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You will indeed taste a painful punishment,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “You surely have to taste the painful punishment.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Now certainly you shall be tasting the painful chastisement,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily ye are going to taste a torment afflictive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, you (pagans of Makkah) are going to taste the painful torment;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Most assuredly, you will taste the painful punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (They will be told): “You shall taste the grievous chastisement.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, you are going to taste the painful torment;

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! (now) verily ye taste the painful doom -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed you will taste the painful punishment,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You shall certainly taste the painful punishment:

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, you [disbelievers] will be tasters of the painful punishment,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (They will be told), "You will certainly suffer the painful torment

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely you will taste the painful punishment.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: you shall surely taste the painful punishment,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Ye shall indeed taste of the Grievous Penalty;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innakum lathaiqoo alAAathabi alaleemi

39.arabic: وَمَا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And be rewarded but only for what you had done,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And you will not be compensated except for your deeds.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and not be recompensed, except according to what you were doing.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And ye shall be required not except for that which ye have been doing;

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you will be requited nothing except for what you used to do (evil deeds, sins, and Allah's disobedience which you used to do in this world);

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And you will be repaid only for what you used to do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You will only be recompensed according to your deeds.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you will be requited nothing except for what you used to do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Ye are requited naught save what ye did -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and you will be requited only for what you used to do

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but you shall not be recompensed except for what you were doing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and will be recompensed only for what you deserve.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you shall not be rewarded except (for) what you did.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and be rewarded only according to your deeds."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But it will be no more than the retribution of (the Evil) that ye have wrought;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama tujzawna illa ma kuntum taAAmaloona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Except the chosen creatures of God,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Except the chosen bondmen of Allah.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Except for God's sincere servants;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: But the bondmen of Allah, the sincere ones -

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Save the chosen slaves of Allah (faithful, obedient, true believers of Islamic Monotheism).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except for God’s sincere servants.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But Allah's chosen servants (shall be spared this woeful end).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Save the chosen servants of Allah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Save single-minded slaves of Allah;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But for the sincere worshipers of Allah,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: But the sincere servants of God

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Save the servants of Allah, the purified ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But the chosen servants of God;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But the sincere (and devoted) Servants of Allah,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa AAibada Allahi almukhlaseena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whose provision is predetermined --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: For them is the sustenance known to Us.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: for them awaits a known provision,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those! theirs shall be a provision known:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: For them there will be a known provision (in Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: For them is a known provision.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: For them awaits a known provision,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: For them there will be a known provision,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: For them there is a known provision,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: For such there is a known provision

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: there is waiting for them a known provision;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those will have a provision determined -

translations.author: Sarwar

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Look, then how was the end of those who had been warned,

translations.text: Therefore see what sort of fate befell those who were warned!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and behold, how was the end of them that were warned,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So behold what wise hath been the end of those who were warned.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then see what was the end of those who were warned (but heeded not).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So observe the end of those who were warned.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Observe, then, what was the end of those that had been warned,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then see what was the end of those who were warned.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then see the nature of the consequence for those warned,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So observe how was the fate of those who were warned

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: See then the end of those who were warned,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then look how was the end of those who were warned -

translations.author: Sarwar

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then see how was the end of those warned,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: See how those who were warned met their end!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then see what was the end of those who were admonished (but heeded not),-

translations.text: Faonthur kayfa kana AAaqibatu almunthareena

translations.text: Other than the chosen creatures of God.

translations.text: Except the chosen bondmen of Allah.

translations.text: except for God's sincere servants.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Save the bondmen of Allah sincere.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except for the sincere servants of God.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: except for the chosen servants of Allah?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Except the chosen servants of Allah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Save single-minded slaves of Allah.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: except the sincere worshipers of Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Only Our sincere servants were saved.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Except the servants of Allah, the purified ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Not so the chosen servants of God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Except the sincere (and devoted) Servants of Allah.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa AAibada Allahi almukhlaseena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Noah had verily called to Us. And how gracious was He who answered (him).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed Nooh prayed to Us – so what an excellent Acceptor of Prayer We are!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Noah called to Us; and how excellent were the Answerers!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly Nuh cried Unto us; and We are the Best of answerers!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed Nuh (Noah) invoked Us, and We are the Best of those who answer (the request).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And Noah called out to Us, and We are the Best of responders.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Noah had called upon Us (earlier). See, how excellent We were in answering him!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed Nuh invoked Us, and We are the best of those who answer.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Noah verily prayed unto Us, and gracious was the Hearer of his prayer

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly Noah called out to Us, and how well did We respond!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Noah called to Us, and We are the Best to answer.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Noah had certainly called Us, and [We are] the best of responders.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Noah called for help. How blessed was the answer which he received.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Nuh did certainly call upon Us, and most excellent answerer of prayer are We.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Noah cried to Us, and how excellent was Our response!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (In the days of old), Noah cried to Us, and We are the best to hear prayer.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad nadana noohun falaniAAma almujeeboona

translations.text: And We saved him and his family from the great calamity.

translations.text: We delivered him and his household from the great calamity;

translations.text: And We rescued him and his family from the great distress,

translations.text: And We saved him and his household from the great distress,

translations.text: And We saved him and his family from the great affliction.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We delivered him and his followers from the mighty distress.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We saved him and his people from great distress,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali

translations.text: Wanajjaynahu waahlahu mina alkarbi alAAatheemi

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and made his offspring the only ones to survive,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And, his progeny, them We made the survivors.

translations.text: And made his seed the survivors,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and made his descendants the survivors,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and We made his offspring the survivors.

translations.text: and We made his offspring the only survivors.

translations.text: And We made his offspring the survivors.

translations.text: and We made his offspring the only survivors.

translations.text: And left (his name) for posterity.

translations.author: Shakir

translations.text: Of his kind was Abraham.

translations.text: Abraham was on the self-same way (as Noah).

translations.text: Of his party was Abraham.

translations.text: And indeed, among his kind was Abraham,

translations.author: Shakir

translations.text: When he came to his Lord with a pure heart,

translations.text: and said to his father and his people: “Whom do you worship?

translations.text: When he said to his father and to his people: "What is it that which you worship"

translations.text: Why do you solicit false gods instead of God?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “What! You desire, through fabrication, Gods other than Allah?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Is it a calumny, gods apart from God, that you desire?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is it a falsehood-god beside Allah - that ye desire?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Is it a falsehood aliha (gods) other than Allah that you seek?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is it falsified gods, instead of God, that you want?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Is it false deities that you want to serve rather than Allah?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Is it a falsehood -- gods other than Allah -- that you desire"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is it a falsehood - gods beside Allah - that ye desire?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Is it a lie, gods other than Allah, that you desire?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It is falsehood that you desire gods other than Allah!

translations.text: Is it falsehood [as] gods other than Allah you desire?

translations.text: Do you want to worship false idols as your lords besides God?

translations.text: A lie-- gods besides Allah-- do you desire?

translations.text: Would you serve false deities instead of God?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Is it a falsehood- gods other than Allah- that ye desire?

translations.text: What think you then of the Lord of all Being?'

translations.text: what, then, is your opinion of the Lord of the worlds?

translations.text: "Then what do you think about the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns, and all that exists)?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So what is your opinion about the Lord of the Worlds?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: What do you think of the Lord of the whole Universe?”

translations.text: "Then what think you about the Lord of the all that exists"

102.arabic: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ ٱلسَّعْىَ قَالَ يَـٰبُنَىَّ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ فِى ٱلْمَنَامِ أَنِّىٓ أَذْبَحُكَ فَٱنظُرْ مَاذَا تَرَىٰ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ ٱفْعَلْ مَا تُؤْمَرُ ۖ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When he was old enough to go about with him, he said: "O my son, I dreamt that I was sacrificing you. Consider, what you think?" He replied: "Father, do as you are commanded. If God pleases you will find me firm."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when he became capable of working with him, Ibrahim said, “O my son, I dreamt that I am sacrificing you – therefore now consider what is your opinion”; he said, “O my father! Do what you are commanded! Allah willing, you will soon find me patiently enduring!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and when he had reached the age of running with him, he said, 'My son, I see in a dream that I shall sacrifice thee; consider, what thinkest thou?' He said, 'My father, do as thou art bidden; thou shalt find me, God willing, one of the steadfast.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when he attained the age of, running with him, he said: O my son! verily I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering thee; so look, what considerest thou? He said: O my father! do that which thou art commanded; thou shalt find me, Allah willing, of the patients.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And, when he (his son) was old enough to walk with him, he said: "O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you (offer you in sacrifice to Allah), so look what you think!" He said: "O my father! Do that which you are commanded, Insha' Allah (if Allah will), you shall find me of As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then, when he was old enough to accompany him, he said, “O My son, I see in a dream that I am sacrificing you; see what you think.” He said, “O my Father, do as you are commanded; you will find me, God willing, one of the steadfast.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and when he was old enough to go about and work with him, (one day) Abraham said to him: “My son, I see in my dream that I am slaughtering you. So consider (and tell me) what you think.” He said: “Do as you are bidden. You will find me, if Allah so wills, among the steadfast.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And, when he (his son) was old enough to walk with him, he said: "O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you. So look what you think!" He said: "O my father! Do that which you are commanded, if Allah wills, you shall find me of the patient."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when (his son) was old enough to walk with him, (Abraham) said: O my dear son, I have seen in a dream that I must sacrifice thee. So look, what thinkest thou? He said: O my father! Do that which thou art commanded. Allah willing, thou shalt find me of the steadfast.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When he was old enough to assist in his endeavour, he said, ‘My son! I see in dreams that I am sacrificing you. See what you think.’ He said, ‘Father! Do whatever you have been commanded. If Allah wishes, you will find me to be patient.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when he reached the age of traveling with him, he said: 'My son, while I was sleeping I saw that I shall slaughter (sacrifice) you, tell me what is your opinion' He replied: 'Father, do as you are ordered (by Allah). Allah willing, you shall find me one of those who are steadfast'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when he reached with him [the age of] exertion, he said, "O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I [must] sacrifice you, so see what you think." He said, "O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When his son was old enough to work with him, he said, "My son, I have had a dream that I must sacrifice you. What do you think of this?" He replied, "Father, fulfill whatever you are commanded to do and you will find me patient, by the will of God".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when he attained to working with him, he said: O my son! surely I have seen in a dream that I should sacrifice you; consider then what you see. He said: O my father! do what you are commanded; if Allah please, you will find me of the patient ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And when he reached the age when he could work with him, he said, "O my son, I have seen in a dream that I am sacrificing you. So tell me what you think of it!" He replied, "O my father, do as you are commanded; and God willing, you will find me steadfast."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then, when (the son) reached (the age of) (serious) work with him, he said: "O my son! I see in vision that I offer thee in sacrifice: Now see what is thy view!" (The son) said: "O my father! Do as thou art commanded: thou will find me, if Allah so wills one practising Patience and Constancy!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falamma balagha maAAahu alssaAAya qala ya bunayya innee ara fee almanami annee athbahuka faonthur matha tara qala ya abati ifAAal ma tumaru satajidunee in shaa Allahu mina alssabireena

113.arabic: وَبَـٰرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَىٰٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ ۚ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا مُحْسِنٌ وَظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ مُبِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among their descendants are some who do good, but some who wrong themselves.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent blessings upon him and Ishaq; and among their descendants – some who do good deeds, and some who clearly wrong themselves.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We blessed him, and Isaac; and of their seed some are good-doers, and some manifest self-wrongers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We blessed him and Is-haq; and of their offspring are some well-doers and some who wrong themselves manifestly.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: We blessed him and Ishaque (Isaac), and of their progeny are (some) that do right, and some that plainly wrong themselves.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We blessed him, and Isaac. But among their descendants are some who are righteous, and some who are clearly unjust to themselves.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among the offspring of the two some did good and some plainly wronged themselves.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: We blessed him and Ishaq. And of their progeny are (some) that do right, and some that plainly wrong themselves.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. And of their seed are some who do good, and some who plainly wrong themselves.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. Among their descendants [some] are virtuous, and [some] who manifestly wrong themselves.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and We blessed him and Isaac, and from their offspring are some gooddoers, and others who clearly wronged themselves.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We blessed him and Isaac. But among their descendants is the doer of good and the clearly unjust to himself.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We had blessed him and Isaac. Some of their offspring were righteous and others were openly unjust to themselves.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We showered Our blessings on him and on Ishaq; and of their offspring are the doers of good, and (also) those who are clearly unjust to their own souls.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and blessed him and Isaac too: some of their offspring were good, but some clearly sinned against their souls.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We blessed him and Isaac: but of their progeny are (some) that do right, and (some) that obviously do wrong, to their own souls.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wabarakna AAalayhi waAAala ishaqa wamin thurriyyatihima muhsinun wathalimun linafsihi mubeenun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We were indeed gracious to Moses and Aaron,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We indeed did a great favour to Moosa and Haroon.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We also favoured Moses and Aaron,

translations.author: Daryabadi

translations.text: And We caused a tree of gourd to grow above him;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So We made a gourd plant grow above him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and We caused a pumpkin tree to grow over him.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We made a plant of gourd grow up for him.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We caused to grow up for him a gourdplant.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and We caused a gourd tree to grow over him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And We caused to grow, over him, a spreading plant of the gourd kind.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waanbatna AAalayhi shajaratan min yaqteenin

147.arabic: وَأَرْسَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ مِا۟ئَةِ أَلْفٍ أَوْ يَزِيدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent him towards a hundred thousand people, in fact more.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then We sent him unto a hundred thousand, or more,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We had sent him to a hundred thousand: rather they exceeded.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (people) or even more.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We sent him to a hundred thousand, or more.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and We sent him forth to a nation of a hundred thousand or more,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (people) or even more.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand (folk) or more

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We sent him to a [community of] hundred thousand or more,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then We sent him to a hundred thousand or more,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We sent him to [his people of] a hundred thousand or more.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent him to a hundred thousand or more people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We sent him to a hundred thousand, rather they exceeded.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent him as a messenger to a hundred thousand people or more,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And We sent him (on a mission) to a hundred thousand (men) or more.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waarsalnahu ila miati alfin aw yazeedoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And they came to believe; so We allowed them to enjoy the good things of life for an age.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So they accepted faith – We therefore gave them usage for a while.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they believed, whrefore We let them enjoy life for a season.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and they believed. So We let them enjoy life for a while.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they believed; so We gave them enjoyment for a while.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they believed, therefor We gave them comfort for a while.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and they believed [in him]. So We provided for them for a while.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and they believed, and We gave them enjoyment for awhile.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment [of life] for a time.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They believed in him so We granted them enjoyment for an appointed time.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they believed, so We gave them provision till a time.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and they believed in him: so We let them live in ease for a while.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And they believed; so We permitted them to enjoy (their life) for a while.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faamanoo famattaAAnahum ila heenin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Now ask them if their Lord has daughters, and they sons?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore ask them (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), whether the daughters are for your Lord and the sons for them!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So ask them for a pronouncement -- Has thy Lord daughters, and they sons?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Now ask thou them: there for thy Lord daughters and for them sons?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Now ask them (O Muhammad SAW): "Are there (only) daughters for your Lord and sons for them?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Ask them, “Are the daughters for your Lord, while for them the sons?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So ask their opinion: “(Are you convinced) that your Lord should have daughters and you should have sons?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Now ask them: "Are there (only) daughters for your Lord and sons for them"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Now ask them (O Muhammad): Hath thy Lord daughters whereas they have sons?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Ask them, are daughters to be for your Lord while sons are to be for them?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Now, ask them, has your Lord daughters, and they sons?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Does your Lord have daughters while they have sons?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Do daughters belong to your Lord and sons to them?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then ask them whether your Lord has daughters and they have sons.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Now ask them whether your Lord has daughters, whereas they have sons.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Now ask them their opinion: Is it that thy Lord has (only) daughters, and they have sons?-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faistaftihim alirabbika albanatu walahumu albanoona

150.arabic: أَمْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ إِنَـٰثًا وَهُمْ شَـٰهِدُونَ

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or created We the angels females while they were witnesses?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or did We create the angels females while they were witnesses?

translations.text: Did We create the angels females while they were present?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
